mnemonics.title: Log Levels Order
mnemonics.content: DIVE WC: Debug → Info → Verbose (sometimes) → Error → Warning → Critical - Remember: 'Dogs In Villages Eat Widgets Casually' (Debug, Info, Error, Warning, Critical)

mnemonics.title: Logging Best Practices
mnemonics.content: SAFER: Sensitive data exclusion, Appropriate levels, Format consistently, Evaluate performance impact, Rotate logs regularly

acronyms.usage: Popular centralized logging and visualization stack
Used by logging frameworks to programmatically control logging
Operations that should be logged for security auditing
Systems that analyze logs for security threats

visualTips.visualization: Think of a pyramid: DEBUG (base, most verbose) → INFO → WARNING → ERROR → CRITICAL (top, least frequent but most serious)
Flow diagram: Application → Logger → Formatter → Appender → Destination (File/Console/Database)
Read from bottom (root cause) to top (where error occurred), like following breadcrumbs backwards

analogies.analogy: Like a ship's black box - records all events for later investigation if something goes wrong
Like a fire alarm system - different sounds for different severity (smoke detector vs fire alarm)
Like rotating tires on a car - distributes wear and prevents any single item from failing
Like a well-organized filing cabinet (JSON) vs a messy pile of papers (plain text)
Like a central control room monitoring multiple security cameras across a building
